data.title: THE SCENE
blocks: You have forty-seven receipts from the department's summer field work. Some are PDFs from vendors with clean digital lay
The Scene — You need a spreadsheet: vendor, date, total, tax, category, and source file for each one.

data.title: EXTRACTION VS. SUMMARIZATION
blocks: Summarization
Extraction
structure confers credibility

data.title: THE SCHEMA COMES FIRST.
blocks: Fields what columns exist, by name
Types text, date, currency, number, category
Source location where in the document this field typically lives
Validation rules what a valid value looks like
Exception rules what to do when the field is missing, ambiguous, unreadable, or uncertain

data.title: A WORKED WALKTHROUGH.
blocks: Vendor text from receipt header; mark "unclear" if unreadable
Date date of purchase from receipt; do not use file metadata as substitute; mark "ambiguous" if uncertain
Total total amount from printed total line; currency in USD; do not infer from line items; mark "uncertain" if unreadable
Tax tax amount if shown; leave blank if absent; do not substitute zero
Category one of ; mark "uncategorized" if unknown

data.title: THE VERIFICATION CHECKLIST.
blocks: Row count first.
Source file column.
Exception log review.
Numeric spot-check.
Totals reconciliation.

data.title: HOW MUCH TO VERIFY.
blocks: Full row-by-row verification
Risk-tiered sampling
Never skip verification entirely.

data.title: THE HUMAN GATE.
blocks: · Row count matches source count (or exceptions explain the difference).
· Every row has a source file reference.
· You have opened and verified a meaningful sample against originals.
· All uncertain fields are either resolved or clearly marked.
· Numeric totals are consistent with any independent cross-check.

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: Skipping the schema.
Treating clean formatting as accuracy.
Using file metadata as a date.
Not requiring an exception log.
Spot-checking only easy rows.

data.title: BRIDGE TO CHAPTER 7.
blocks: You have extracted the data. Now someone needs to read it.
Bridge to Chapter 7 — The next chapter covers the other direction of Cowork document work: not taking structured data out of documents, but as

blocks: Katherine Johnson
Run this:
"Katherine Johnson mathematician"
